name: La Baronia de Maldà — Circular Route PR-C 57
name_original: La Baronia de Maldà — Sendero Circular PR-C 57
region_intro: Hiking around Sant Martí De Riucorb offers exploration through a landscape characterized by the Corb River valley, extensive agricultural fields of cereals, olive trees, vineyards, and almond trees. The area includes hilly terrain and portions of the Secans de Belianes-Preixana Protected Natural Space, providing varied environments for outdoor activities. Medieval villages and historical elements like ancient castles and Romanesque chapels are integrated into the natural setting. Best hiking trails around Sant Martí De Riucorb The most popular hiking route is La Baronia de Maldà — Circular Route PR-C 57, a 9.8 miles (15.7 km) trail that takes 4 hours 15 minutes to complete. This moderate route winds through agricultural landscapes and past historical villages. Another top favourite among local hikers is Hiking loop from Vallbona de les Monges, an easy 4.1 miles (6.5 km) path. This trail offers views of the Corb River valley and surrounding cultivated lands. Local hikers also love the Santuario del Tallat – Montblanquet loop from Montblanquet, a 5.9 miles (9.5 km) trail leading through hilly terrain and agricultural areas, often completed in about 2 hours 42 minutes. Hiking around Sant Martí De Riucorb is defined by the Corb River valley, extensive agricultural fields, and hilly terrain, with options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes. The routes in Sant Martí De Riucorb are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from 18 reviews. More than 100 hikers have used komoot to explore Sant Martí De Riucorb's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Sant Martí De Riucorb?
A: There are over 30 hiking routes in Sant Martí De Riucorb, offering a variety of experiences. These include 8 easy trails, 24 moderate routes, and 2 more challenging options, ensuring something for every hiker.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes in Sant Martí De Riucorb?
A: The terrain in Sant Martí De Riucorb is diverse, characterized by the Corb River valley, extensive agricultural fields of cereals, olive trees, vineyards, and almond trees. You'll also encounter hilly sections, particularly around the medieval villages, and parts of the Secans de Belianes-Preixana Protected Natural Space.

Q: Are there easy hiking options suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, Sant Martí De Riucorb offers several easy hiking options perfect for beginners or families. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14944779" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hiking loop from Vallbona de les Monges&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 4.1-mile (6.5 km) path that provides pleasant views of the Corb River valley and cultivated lands.

Q: What natural features define the hiking experience in Sant Martí De Riucorb?
A: The hiking experience is largely defined by the serene Corb River valley, the picturesque mosaic of agricultural fields (cereals, olive, almond, and vineyards), and the gently rolling, hilly terrain. A significant portion of the municipality also extends into the Secans de Belianes-Preixana Protected Natural Space, offering opportunities to observe local wildlife.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks or villages to explore on the trails?
A: Absolutely. The area is rich in history. You can explore medieval villages like Sant Martí de Maldà, Rocafort de Vallbona, Llorenç de Rocafort, and Vilet, with their stone houses and ancient streets. Remnants of castles, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5054639" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Maldà&lt;/a&gt;, Romanesque chapels, and boundary crosses add significant historical interest to many routes.

Q: Can I find any waterfalls while hiking in the area?
A: Yes, there is a notable waterfall nearby. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4591004" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salt del Duran Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; is an attraction you can visit, offering a refreshing natural highlight in the region.

Q: Are the hiking trails in Sant Martí De Riucorb suitable for dogs?
A: Many trails in Sant Martí De Riucorb are suitable for dogs, especially those traversing agricultural lands and rural paths. However, it's always recommended to keep dogs on a leash, particularly when passing through cultivated areas or near livestock, and to respect local regulations.

Q: Are there circular hiking routes available?
A: Yes, circular routes are a popular way to explore the area. A great example is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e936987674" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Baronia de Maldà — Circular Route PR-C 57&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 9.8-mile (15.7 km) trail that takes you through agricultural landscapes and past historical villages.

Q: What are the parking options for trailheads?
A: Parking is generally available in or near the villages that serve as starting points for many trails, such as Sant Martí de Maldà or Montblanquet. As the area is rural, roadside parking or small village car parks are common. Specific parking details can often be found on individual route pages.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the trails in Sant Martí De Riucorb?
A: The trails in Sant Martí De Riucorb are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from 18 reviews. Hikers often praise the peaceful rural atmosphere, the picturesque agricultural landscapes, and the charm of the medieval villages encountered along the routes.

Q: What is the best time of year to hike in Sant Martí De Riucorb?
A: Spring and autumn are generally the best times for hiking in Sant Martí De Riucorb. During these seasons, the weather is mild and pleasant, and the landscape is particularly beautiful, with blooming fields in spring and vibrant colors in autumn. Summers can be hot, while winters are cooler but often still suitable for walking.

Q: Where can I find places to eat or stay near the hiking routes?
A: The small villages within Sant Martí De Riucorb, such as Sant Martí de Maldà, Rocafort de Vallbona, and Llorenç de Rocafort, offer local establishments where you might find traditional Catalan cuisine or rural accommodation options. For a wider selection, nearby larger towns would be a good option.

Q: Are there any castles I can visit while hiking?
A: Yes, the region has several historical castles. You can visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5054639" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Maldà&lt;/a&gt;, or explore other nearby castles like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5197307" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arbeca Castle&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5198761" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bellpuig Castle&lt;/a&gt;, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5198834" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Verdú Castle&lt;/a&gt;, which are all within reach and add a historical dimension to your outdoor adventure.
tips: XIII century castle located in the upper part of Maldà. Only the exterior is preserved. Gothic and medieval style.
Religious building, 16th-17th century. Baroque style and located in the main square of the town of Sant Martí de Maldà. It is a Cultural Asset of National Interest (BCIN).

waypoints.name: Church of Sant Martí de Maldà
